title: Clock supervisor harness for periodic restart
description: Run kanbus_clock under a supervisor that restarts every 7 minutes for realtime kbs testing.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Implementing supervisor harness to restart kanbus_clock every 7 minutes and launching it for realtime testing.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Supervisor launched successfully. Running kanbus_clock under periodic restart every 420s. Active PIDs: supervisor=67890, clock=67923.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Switching from periodic restart supervisor to continuous single-process kanbus_clock runtime per request.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Supervisor stopped. Running continuous single kanbus_clock process (no periodic restart).

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Restarting continuous kanbus_clock after config change.

comments.author: unknown
comments.text: Patched gossip ingest to reduce permanent backlog: (1) timestamp fallback to issue.updated_at/created_at when envelope ts is missing, (2) cross-root stable-signature dedupe to suppress replay duplicates with different envelope ids/producers/projects. Tests passing (62).

comments.author: unknown
comments.text: Investigating repeated old notifications (e.g., Task A). Applying stricter startup cutoff handling for gossip events with missing timestamps to prevent replayed historical events from being enqueued.

comments.author: unknown
comments.text: Applied strict startup cutoff fix: gossip events with missing timestamps are now skipped when startup_cutoff is active, preventing replayed stale notifications from entering the queue. Restarted clock with this patch.

comments.author: unknown
comments.text: Implementing adaptive notification hold duration: 30s at empty queue, linearly down to 10s at depth>=10, minimum 10s.

comments.author: unknown
comments.text: Reverting temporary hook/noise special-case filter per request. Continuing focused FPS investigation on React clock render path.
